sections.title: Materials & Properties
sections.instructions: Listen to the sentence, then answer the question about it.

questions.sentence: The scarf is made of wool.
questions.question: What is the scarf made of?

questions.sentence: The crown is made of gold. It's very heavy.
questions.question: Why is the crown heavy?

questions.sentence: Be careful with that vase! It's made of glass.
questions.question: Why should you be careful?

questions.sentence: Rubber is flexible. You can bend it easily.
questions.question: What property does rubber have?

questions.sentence: This table is rigid. It doesn't bend at all.
questions.question: What happens if you try to bend the table?

questions.sentence: The newspaper is very light. You can carry ten of them.
questions.question: What is true about newspapers?

questions.sentence: The wings for the costume are made of paper.
questions.question: What material are the wings?

questions.sentence: My ring is made of silver. It's smooth and shiny.
questions.question: How does the ring feel?

questions.sentence: Bricks are rough and heavy. They're used to build walls.
questions.question: Which TWO properties do bricks have?

questions.sentence: Plastic is a good material for toys because it is light and flexible.
questions.question: Why is plastic good for toys?

questions.sentence: We need to use safe materials for the school play.
questions.question: What kind of materials should they use?

questions.sentence: The eraser is made of rubber. That's why it bends.
questions.question: Why does the eraser bend?

sections.title: Making Suggestions
sections.instructions: Listen to the sentence, then answer the question about it.

questions.sentence: Let's go to the cinema tonight!
questions.question: What does the speaker want to do?

questions.sentence: Shall we make costumes for the play?
questions.question: What is the speaker suggesting?

questions.sentence: We could use blue paper for the sea.
questions.question: What is the blue paper for?

questions.sentence: Shall we design some props for the show?
questions.question: What does the speaker want to design?

questions.sentence: Let's paint the clouds with white paint.
questions.question: What colour will the clouds be?

questions.sentence: We could buy Julia a ring for her birthday.
questions.question: Who is the gift for?

questions.sentence: Shall we recycle these old T-shirts for costumes?
questions.question: What will they do with old T-shirts?

questions.sentence: Let's start the performance now!
questions.question: When does the speaker want to start?

questions.sentence: We could ask the teacher for help with the set.
questions.question: Who might help them?

questions.sentence: Shall we have a party after the show?
questions.question: When is the party?

sections.title: Directions & Places
sections.instructions: Listen to the sentence, then answer the question about it.

questions.sentence: Go straight on and turn left at the traffic lights.
questions.question: Where do you turn left?

questions.sentence: The bank is next to the supermarket.
questions.question: Where is the bank?

questions.sentence: Walk past the park and turn right.
questions.question: What do you do after the park?

questions.sentence: The cinema is opposite the restaurant.
questions.question: Where is the cinema?

questions.sentence: Go through the tunnel to reach the station.
questions.question: How do you get to the station?

questions.sentence: The library is between the school and the post office.
questions.question: What is on either side of the library?

questions.sentence: Take the first turning on the left.
questions.question: Which turning should you take?

questions.sentence: Walk along this street for about two hundred metres.
questions.question: How far should you walk?

questions.sentence: Go round the roundabout and take the second exit.
questions.question: Which exit at the roundabout?

questions.sentence: The hospital is at the end of this road.
questions.question: Where is the hospital?
options: At the end of the road
At the start of the road
questions.answer: At the end of the road

questions.sentence: The museum is next to the town hall.
questions.question: What is next to the town hall?

questions.sentence: Walk across the bridge to get to the park.
questions.question: What do you cross to reach the park?

sections.title: Rules & Advice
sections.instructions: Listen to the sentence, then answer the question about it.

questions.sentence: You must wear a school uniform.
questions.question: Is wearing a uniform optional?

questions.sentence: You should eat more fruit.
questions.question: Is this a rule or advice?

questions.sentence: You mustn't use your phone in class.
questions.question: Can you use your phone in class?

questions.sentence: We have to get up early tomorrow. The bus leaves at six.
questions.question: Why must they get up early?

questions.sentence: You can't park here. It's a no-parking zone.
questions.question: Why can't you park here?

questions.sentence: You should take an umbrella. It might rain.
questions.question: Why should you take an umbrella?

questions.sentence: Students must be quiet in the library.
questions.question: What must students do in the library?

questions.sentence: You mustn't swim in this lake. It's too dangerous.
questions.question: Why mustn't you swim?

questions.sentence: Everyone must have a passport to travel abroad.
questions.question: What do you need to travel abroad?

questions.sentence: I think you should say sorry to her.
questions.question: What is the speaker suggesting?

sections.title: Technology
sections.instructions: Listen to the sentence, then answer the question about it.

questions.sentence: I need to download this file from the Internet to my computer.
questions.question: Where is the file going?

questions.sentence: Open your browser and type the address in the bar.
questions.question: What should you open first?

questions.sentence: You need a password to log in to your account.
questions.question: What do you need to log in?

questions.sentence: She put all her books in her rucksack before leaving.
questions.question: Where did she put her books?

questions.sentence: You need wifi to connect to the Internet without a cable.
questions.question: What is wifi for?

questions.sentence: Can you copy this text and paste it into the other document?
questions.question: What two actions are needed?

questions.sentence: Don't forget to bring your charger or your phone battery will die.
questions.question: What happens without the charger?

questions.sentence: Use a search engine to find information online.
questions.question: What is a search engine for?

questions.sentence: The keyboard and mouse are the main input devices on a computer.
questions.question: What are the keyboard and mouse?

questions.sentence: I read the novel on my e-book during the train journey.
questions.question: How did they read the novel?

sections.title: Jobs
sections.instructions: Listen to the sentence, then answer the question about it.

questions.sentence: The waiter brought us our drinks and took our food order.
questions.question: Where does this person work?

questions.sentence: She wants to be an actor when she grows up because she loves performing on stage.
questions.question: What does she love?

questions.sentence: The cook made a delicious meal for fifty guests.
questions.question: How many guests did he cook for?

questions.sentence: The journalist wrote an article about the school play for the newspaper.
questions.question: What did she write about?

questions.sentence: He is a photographer. He takes photos of nature and animals.
questions.question: What does he photograph?

questions.sentence: The designer created a new costume for the school musical.
questions.question: What did the designer create?

questions.sentence: The singer performed three songs at the concert last night.
questions.question: How many songs did she sing?

questions.sentence: The acrobat performed amazing tricks high above the audience.
questions.question: Where did the acrobat perform?

questions.sentence: My grandpa is a farmer. He grows strawberries and keeps chickens.
questions.question: What animals does grandpa keep?

questions.sentence: The driver took us to the airport in thirty minutes.
questions.question: How long was the journey?

questions.sentence: The artist painted a portrait of the queen last year.
questions.question: What did the artist paint?

questions.sentence: The photographer uses a camera with a very long lens.
questions.question: What kind of lens does she use?

sections.title: Appearance & Personality
sections.instructions: Listen to the sentence, then answer the question about it.

questions.sentence: He's got short brown hair and blue eyes.
questions.question: What colour are his eyes?

questions.sentence: She's very clever and interesting.
questions.question: Is this about appearance or personality?

questions.sentence: He likes swimming and cycling in the park.
questions.question: What does he enjoy?

questions.sentence: My brother is very lazy. He never does his homework.
questions.question: Why is he called lazy?

questions.sentence: She is popular and brave. Everyone likes her.
questions.question: How do people feel about her?

questions.sentence: What does your grandad look like? He's tall with grey hair.
questions.question: What is being asked about?

questions.sentence: She's unfriendly. She doesn't talk to anyone.
questions.question: Does she talk to people?

questions.sentence: He's kind. He always helps his classmates.
questions.question: How does he help others?

questions.sentence: My cat is lovely. It always sits on my lap.
questions.question: Where does the cat sit?

questions.sentence: What is your teacher like? She's friendly and clever.
questions.question: What type of question is this?

sections.title: Zero Conditional
sections.instructions: Listen to the sentence, then answer the question about it.

questions.sentence: If you heat ice, it melts.
questions.question: What happens to ice when you heat it?

questions.sentence: When water reaches one hundred degrees, it boils.
questions.question: At what temperature does water boil?

questions.sentence: If you don't water a plant, it dies.
questions.question: What does a plant need to survive?

questions.sentence: When the sun goes down, it gets dark.
questions.question: When does it get dark?

questions.sentence: If you study hard, you get good marks.
questions.question: How do you get good marks?

questions.sentence: When it rains, the streets get wet.
questions.question: What makes the streets wet?

questions.sentence: If you eat too much chocolate, you feel sick.
questions.question: What causes the sick feeling?

questions.sentence: If you press the button, the door opens.
questions.question: What opens the door?

questions.sentence: When a baby is hungry, it cries.
questions.question: Why does the baby cry?

questions.sentence: If you mix water and oil, they don't mix.
questions.question: Can water and oil mix?
